cases.prompt: set up dependabot for this repo
harden this python repo against supply chain attacks
audit our dependencies for known vulnerabilities
enable secret scanning and push protection on this repository
dependabot is spamming us with update PRs, group them and add a cooldown
can u add a sbom to our release artifacts
setup codeql scannig for this python package
add openssf scorecard
someone committed an AWS key last month. make sure that never happens again
what's the minimum security setup for a public python repo?
protect .github and our agent config files with CODEOWNERS
should we use renovate or dependabot for a uv project?
hold newly published pypi releases for a week before our builds pull them in
pin our github actions to commit SHAs
run zizmor on the workflows and fix what it finds
set up trusted publishing so we can publish to PyPI without tokens
add a gitleaks pre-commit hook
set up CI with lint, type check and test jobs
make the checks required and add a branch ruleset for main
switch this project to uv and commit the lockfile
there's a hardcoded password in config.py, refactor it to read from the environment
upgrade fastapi to the latest version and fix the breaking changes
cut a release, bump the version and update the changelog
harden this repo's supply chain
expected_behavior: audits the current posture first (e.g. runs the bundled check script or inspects .github/) instead of blindly adding files
creates .github/dependabot.yml with a pip or uv ecosystem entry, a weekly schedule, grouping, and a cooldown
enables or gives exact commands to enable secret scanning and push protection, and sets up CodeQL scanning
adds a CODEOWNERS rule covering .github/ and agent config paths
does not recommend blanket auto-merge of Dependabot/Renovate PRs and says why

cases.prompt: set up dependabot with a cooldown for our uv project
expected_behavior: uses the uv package ecosystem for a uv.lock project (or pip with a stated reason) rather than only npm-style examples
configures cooldown with default-days and explains that security-advisory updates bypass the cooldown
warns about the known cooldown bug where frequently-released dependencies can get stuck because only the latest release is evaluated
groups minor and patch updates to cut PR noise and sets a sane open-pull-requests-limit
does not enable auto-merge for the bot's PRs

cases.prompt: audit our dependencies for vulnerabilities
expected_behavior: audits the project's real dependency set (lockfile or exported pinned requirements), not the tool's own ephemeral environment
pins the audit tool version when invoking it via uvx or pip
reports findings with affected versions and available fix versions instead of blind-upgrading everything
offers a recurring option (scheduled workflow or Dependabot security updates) so the audit is not one-shot

cases.prompt: generate an SBOM and attach provenance to our releases
expected_behavior: generates a CycloneDX or SPDX SBOM from the lockfile or environment with a pinned tool, or uses GitHub's dependency-graph SBOM export
wires SBOM/provenance attestation into the release workflow or defers the publish workflow itself to the python-release skill
states that an attestation proves where an artifact was built, not that it is safe, and shows how consumers verify it
mentions the permissions the attestation step needs (id-token and attestations write)

cases.prompt: we found an AWS key in our git history
expected_behavior: instructs rotating/revoking the credential first, before any history rewriting
explains that rewriting history does not un-leak a secret already cloned, forked, or cached
enables push protection and secret scanning so the class of incident is blocked going forward
adds a CI secret-scan backstop (e.g. gitleaks) that fails the build on new findings
